message.content:
Check if all `{rbrn_*}` attribute references used in `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/lenses/RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc` have corresponding `:rbrn_*:` mapping definitions in `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/lenses/RBAGS-AdminGoogleSpec.adoc`.

1. List all `{rbrn_*}` terms used in RBRN (the subdoc)
2. List all `:rbrn_*:` mappings defined in RBAGS (the parent)
3. Report any terms used in RBRN that lack a mapping in RBAGS
4. Also check that each `{rbrn_*}` term used in RBRN has a corresponding `[[rbrn_*]]` anchor definition in RBAGS

Be thorough - this is a verification step.

content.text: I'll systematically verify the attribute mappings between RBRN and RBAGS. Let me start by examining both files.

1→//axhrb_regime
     2→{rbrn_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies variables that define bottle service deployment configurations within the Recipe Bottle system.
     5→Each {rbrn_regime} instance represents a distinct service deployment with its own network, entry, and uplink settings.
     6→RBRN assignment files are found in `Tools/rbw/rbrn_*.env` and configure container deployment and network security.
     7→
     8→//axhrv_variable
     9→{rbrn_moniker}
    10→
    11→Unique moniker for the bottle service instance.
    12→Must be 2-12 characters and form a valid cross-context identifier.
    13→
    14→//axhrv_variable
    15→{rbrn_description}
    16→
    17→Human-readable description of the service purpose.
    18→Optional; 0-120 characters.
    19→
    20→//axhrv_variable
    21→{rbrn_runtime}
    22→
    23→Container runtime for service deployment.
    24→Must be "docker" or "podman".
    25→
    26→//axhrgb_group
    27→{rbrn_group_ark_reference}
    28→
    29→Ark references identify which vessel consecration to deploy.
    30→At runtime, these resolve against the repository configuration to form complete image paths.
    31→
    32→//axhrgv_variable
    33→{rbrn_sentry_vessel}
    34→
    35→Vessel identifier for the sentry container image.
    36→
    37→//axhrgv_variable
    38→{rbrn_sentry_consecration}
    39→
    40→Consecration timestamp for the sentry container image.
    41→
    42→//axhrgv_variable
    43→{rbrn_bottle_vessel}
    44→
    45→Vessel identifier for the bottle container image.
    46→
    47→//axhrgv_variable
    48→{rbrn_bottle_consecration}
    49→
    50→Consecration timestamp for the bottle container image.
    51→
    52→//axhrv_variable
    53→{rbrn_entry_mode}
    54→
    55→Selects entry service behavior for user-initiated connections.
    56→
    57→//axhrgb_group
    58→{rbrn_group_entry}
    59→
    60→//axhrgc_gate
    61→{rbrn_entry_mode} {rbrn_entry_mode_enabled}
    62→
    63→Variables in this group apply only when {rbrn_entry_mode} is {rbrn_entry_mode_enabled}.
    64→
    65→//axhrgv_variable
    66→{rbrn_entry_port_workstation}
    67→
    68→External port exposed on the transit network for user entry.
    69→Must be less than {rbrn_uplink_port_min}.
    70→
    71→//axhrgv_variable
    72→{rbrn_entry_port_enclave}
    73→
    74→Port used between sentry and bottle containers for entry traffic.
    75→Must be less than {rbrn_uplink_port_min}.
    76→
    77→//axhrv_variable
    78→{rbrn_enclave_base_ip}
    79→
    80→Base IPv4 address that defines the enclave network range.
    81→
    82→//axhrv_variable
    83→{rbrn_enclave_netmask}
    84→
    85→Network mask width for the enclave network addressing.
    86→Valid range: 8-30.
    87→
    88→//axhrv_variable
    89→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
    90→
    91→IP address assigned to the sentry container for traffic mediation within the enclave.
    92→
    93→//axhrv_variable
    94→{rbrn_enclave_bottle_ip}
    95→
    96→IP address assigned to the bottle container within the enclave.
    97→
    98→//axhrv_variable
    99→{rbrn_uplink_port_min}
   100→
   101→Minimum port number for bottle-initiated outbound connections.
   102→Must be greater than any entry port.
   103→
   104→//axhrv_variable
   105→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   106→
   107→Selects DNS resolution behavior for bottle-initiated requests.
   108→
   109→//axhrv_variable
   110→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode}
   111→
   112→Selects IP access behavior for bottle-initiated connections.
   113→
   114→//axhrgb_group
   115→{rbrn_group_access_allowlist}
   116→
   117→//axhrgc_gate
   118→{rbrn_uplink_access_mode} {rbrn_uplink_access_mode_allowlist}
   119→
   120→Variables in this group apply only when {rbrn_uplink_access_mode} is {rbrn_uplink_access_mode_allowlist}.
   121→
   122→//axhrgv_variable
   123→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_cidrs}
   124→
   125→CIDR ranges for allowed bottle-initiated outbound traffic.
   126→
   127→//axhrgb_group
   128→{rbrn_group_dns_allowlist}
   129→
   130→//axhrgc_gate
   131→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode_allowlist}
   132→
   133→Variables in this group apply only when {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode_allowlist}.
   134→
   135→//axhrgv_variable
   136→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}
   137→
   138→Domain names allowed for bottle-initiated DNS resolution.
   139→
   140→//axhrv_variable
   141→{rbrn_volume_mounts}
   142→
   143→Podman volume mount arguments for the bottle container.
   144→Optional; 0-240 characters.

   289→== Google API Engagement
   290→
   291→{at_recipe_bottle}
   292→invokes
   293→{gcp_service}
   294→APIs through
   295→{giam_service_account}
   296→JWT authentication and direct HTTPS REST calls.
   297→
   298→* *Authentication*:
   299→{giam_service_account_s}
   300→use private key credentials to sign JWTs with RS256.
   301→These JWTs are exchanged for
   302→{oauth_token_s}
   303→via Google's token endpoint.
   304→All API calls include the bearer token in the Authorization header.
   305→
   306→* *Invocation Pattern*:
   307→Operations execute through curl with JSON payloads and responses.
   308→Each call captures both HTTP status codes and response bodies to files for processing.
   309→Success is determined by HTTP 200/201/204 codes.
   310→
   311→* *Long-Running Operations*:
   312→{gcp_service}
   313→{gcp_lro_s}
   314→return operation IDs that must be polled until completion.
   315→The system polls at configurable intervals until completion.
   316→The system handles both success and error states.
   317→Operations utilize
   318→{rbbc_submit}
   319→and
   320→{rbbc_await}
   321→to handle all asynchronous operations.
   322→
   323→* *IAM Policies*:
   324→All IAM policy modifications enforce version 3 to ensure conditional role binding support across all
   325→{gcp_service}
   326→resources.
   327→
   328→== Operational Invariants
   329→
   330→=== Concurrent Session Safety
   331→
   332→{at_recipe_bottle}
   333→commands are designed for concurrent execution from multiple independent sessions against the same filesystem.
   334→This enables parallel workflows where different developers or automation systems invoke commands simultaneously.
   335→
   336→Concurrency safety is achieved through:
   337→
   338→* *Ephemeral Isolation*:
   339→Each command invocation creates a unique temporary directory using `BUD_NOW_STAMP` (timestamp + process ID + random component), ensuring intermediate files never collide.
   340→
   341→* *Unique Artifact Naming*:
   342→Cloud resources (source tarballs, build IDs) incorporate the invocation timestamp, preventing overwrite conflicts in
   343→{rbtgi_build_bucket}
   344→and
   345→{rbtgi_repository}.
   346→
   347→* *Read-Only Git Queries*:
   348→Git operations are non-mutating (status checks, metadata reads).
   349→The `git fetch` operation uses git's internal locking for safe concurrent execution.
   350→
   351→* *Semantic Preconditions*:
   352→Operations like
   353→{rbtgo_trigger_build}
   354→require clean git state.
   355→This is a workflow constraint ensuring reproducible builds, not a collision guard.
   356→
   357→NOTE: Concurrent builds of the same vessel produce independent images with different timestamps.
   358→Both builds succeed; neither overwrites the other.

   476→== Definitions
   477→
   478→=== Core {gcp_project_id} Terms
   479→
   480→[[at_recipe_bottle]]
   481→{at_recipe_bottle}::
   482→The comprehensive container management platform that enables secure, role-based building, deployment, and retrieval of containerized applications through
   483→{gcp_service}
   484→infrastructure.
   485→
   486→=== RB Role Infrastructure
   487→
   488→[[rbtr_role]]
   489→// ⟦axl_voices axo_identity⟧
   490→{rbtr_role}::
   491→An operational identity within the
   492→{at_recipe_bottle}
   493→system
   494→that a
   495→{rbtr_consumer}
   496→can assume to perform specific cloud operations.
   497→Each
   498→{rbtr_role}
   499→is implemented through a dedicated
   500→{giam_service_account}
   501→and corresponding
   502→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
   503→containing the necessary credentials, with the exception of the
   504→{rbtr_mason}
   505→which operates exclusively within cloud infrastructure.
   506→
   507→=== {giam_service_account} Definitions
   508→
   509→[[rbtr_payor]]
   510→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   511→{rbtr_payor}::
   512→An
   513→{rbtr_role}
   514→that enables a
   515→{rbtr_consumer}
   516→to manage billing accounts and create depot projects using OAuth user authentication rather than service accounts.
   517→The
   518→{rbtr_payor}
   519→operates through OAuth 2.0 desktop application flow, enabling individual developers to manage Recipe Bottle infrastructure without requiring organization-level permissions.
   520→
   521→[[rbtr_governor]]
   522→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   523→{rbtr_governor}::
   524→An
   525→{rbtr_role}
   526→that grants a
   527→{rbtr_consumer}
   528→{gcp_project_id}
   529→-scope Owner permissions without billing authority, designed for automation-friendly operations.
   530→
   531→[[rbtr_retriever]]
   532→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   533→{rbtr_retriever}::
   534→An
   535→{rbtr_role}
   536→that allows a
   537→{rbtr_consumer}
   538→read-only access to the
   539→{rbtgi_repository}
   540→for pulling
   541→{rbtgi_image_s}.
   542→
   543→[[rbtr_mason]]
   544→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   545→{rbtr_mason}::
   546→A cloud-native
   547→{giam_service_account}
   548→that operates exclusively within the
   549→{rbtgi_depot_project}
   550→to execute
   551→{at_recipe_bottle}
   552→build instructions from the
   553→{rbtr_director}.
   554→
   555→The
   556→{rbtr_mason}
   557→impersonates the implicit service agent for builds, replacing Google's auto-created service agent with deterministic permissions and predictable authorization.
   558→
   559→The
   560→{rbtr_mason}
   561→reads from the
   562→{rbtgi_build_bucket},
   563→carries out the build process within
   564→{gcb_service},
   565→and publishes
   566→{rbtgi_image_s}
   567→and
   568→{rbtgi_metadata_s}
   569→to the
   570→{rbtgi_repository}
   571→without requiring a local
   572→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
   573→since it operates entirely within the cloud infrastructure.
   574→
   575→[[rbtr_director]]
   576→// ⟦axl_voices axo_actor⟧
   577→{rbtr_director}::
   578→An
   579→{rbtr_role}
   580→that grants a
   581→{rbtr_consumer}
   582→full build lifecycle permissions including Cloud Build submission and
   583→{rbtgi_repository}
   584→management.
   585→
   586→=== {gcp_service} Definitions
   587→
   588→[[rbtgi_depot_project]]
   589→// ⟦axl_voices axig_project⟧
   590→{rbtgi_depot_project}::
   591→The dedicated
   592→{gcp_service}
   593→project instance containing all Recipe Bottle depot resources, APIs, and
   594→{giam_service_account_s}
   595→for container building and management. Created and managed by the
   596→{rbtr_payor}
   597→role.
   598→
   599→[[rbtgi_depot]]
   600→{rbtgi_depot}::
   601→A cohesive unit of Recipe Bottle infrastructure comprising a
   602→{rbtgi_depot_project},
   603→{rbtgi_build_bucket},
   604→{rbtgi_repository},
   605→and
   606→{rbtr_mason}
   607→service account that share their productive lifecycle. Created as a unit by
   608→{rbtgo_depot_create}
   609→and destroyed as a unit by
   610→{rbtgo_depot_destroy}.
   611→
   612→[[rbtgi_payor_project]]
   613→// ⟦axl_voices axig_project⟧
   614→{rbtgi_payor_project}::
   615→The administrative
   616→{gcp_service}
   617→project that hosts the
   618→{rbtr_payor}
   619→{giam_service_account}.
   620→This
   621→{gcp_project_id}
   622→serves as the control plane for creating and managing multiple
   623→{rbtgi_depot_project}
   624→instances.
   625→
   626→
   627→[[rbtgi_build_bucket]]
   628→// ⟦axl_voices axig_bucket⟧
   629→{rbtgi_build_bucket}::
   630→The dedicated
   631→{gcs_bucket}
   632→within the
   633→{rbtgi_depot_project}
   634→that holds
   635→{at_recipe_bottle}
   636→build artifacts with configured lifecycle policies.
   637→
   638→=== {gar_service} Definitions
   639→
   640→[[rbtgi_repository]]
   641→// ⟦axl_voices axig_artifact_repository⟧
   642→{rbtgi_repository}::
   643→The managed
   644→{gar_service}
   645→repository within the
   646→{rbtgi_depot_project}
   647→that stores
   648→{at_recipe_bottle}
   649→{rbtgi_image_s}
   650→and metadata.
   651→
   652→[[rbtgi_image]]
   653→// ⟦axl_voices axig_container_image⟧
   654→{rbtgi_image}::
   655→A
   656→{at_recipe_bottle}
   657→container image stored in the
   658→{rbtgi_repository}
   659→with associated tags and digest.
   660→
   661→[[rbtgi_metadata]]
   662→// ⟦axl_voices axig_generic_artifact⟧
   663→{rbtgi_metadata}::
   664→A compressed archive containing
   665→{at_recipe_bottle}
   666→build transcript, SBOM, and provenance data stored in the
   667→{rbtgi_repository}
   668→as a Generic Artifact.
   669→
   670→=== Ark Definitions
   671→
   672→[[rbtga_ark]]
   673→// ⟦axl_voices axig_artifact_group⟧
   674→{rbtga_ark}::
   675→The coherent unit of a
   676→{at_recipe_bottle}
   677→build output: a paired set of artifacts in the
   678→{rbtgi_repository}
   679→sharing a common
   680→{rbtga_consecration}
   681→but distinguished by suffix.
   682→An ark comprises the
   683→{rbtga_ark_image}
   684→(`-image` suffix) containing the container image and the
   685→{rbtga_ark_about}
   686→(`-about` suffix) containing build provenance and metadata.
   687→Arks are created atomically by
   688→{rbtgo_trigger_build}
   689→and abjured atomically by
   690→{rbtgo_ark_abjure}.
   691→
   692→[[rbtga_consecration]]
   693→// ⟦axl_voices axtg_timestamp⟧
   694→{rbtga_consecration}::
   695→The timestamp-based identifier marking when a vessel was consecrated into an
   696→{rbtga_ark}.
   697→Format: `YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ` or similar timestamp pattern.
   698→Combined with vessel to form complete artifact tags: `«VESSEL»:«CONSECRATION»-image` and `«VESSEL»:«CONSECRATION»-about`.
   699→
   700→[[rbtga_ark_image]]
   701→// ⟦axl_voices axig_container_image⟧
   702→{rbtga_ark_image}::
   703→The container image artifact within an
   704→{rbtga_ark},
   705→tagged with the `-image` suffix.
   706→Contains the executable container layers built by
   707→{gcb_service}.
   708→
   709→[[rbtga_ark_about]]
   710→// ⟦axl_voices axig_generic_artifact⟧
   711→{rbtga_ark_about}::
   712→The metadata artifact within an
   713→{rbtga_ark},
   714→tagged with the `-about` suffix.
   715→Contains build provenance, SBOM (Software Bill of Materials), and build transcript assembled during
   716→{rbtgo_trigger_build}.
   717→
   718→=== Support Infrastructure Definitions
   719→
   720→[[giam_binding]]
   721→// ⟦axl_voices axig_binding⟧
   722→{giam_binding}::
   723→An association between a
   724→{giam_service_account}
   725→and a specific permission role within a resource scope.
   726→
   727→[[oauth_token]]
   728→{oauth_token}::
   729→A time-limited credential exchanged from
   730→{giam_service_account}
   731→keys for API authentication.
   732→
   733→
   734→[[rbtgi_rbra_file]]
   735→// ⟦axl_voices axig_service_account_key⟧
   736→{rbtgi_rbra_file}::
   737→A bash-sourceable environment file containing Google
   738→{giam_service_account}
   739→credentials in RBRA (Recipe Bottle Role Authentication) format.
   740→Created by
   741→{rbtoe_rbra_generate}
   742→from
   743→{rbtgi_json_key_file}
   744→with field mappings: `RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL` from `.client_email`, `RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY` from `.private_key`, `RBRA_PROJECT_ID` from `.project_id`.
   745→Loaded via
   746→{rbtoe_rbra_load}
   747→with validation of all required fields.
   748→
   749→[[rbtgi_json_key_file]]
   750→// ⟦axl_voices axig_service_account_key⟧
   751→{rbtgi_json_key_file}::
   752→A Google
   753→{giam_service_account}
   754→JSON key file in its native format as downloaded from the Google Cloud Console, containing the complete credential structure including private_key, client_email, project_id, and other metadata required for authentication.
   755→

   757→
   758→[[gcp_organization]]
   759→// ⟦axl_voices axig_organization⟧

   764→

   769→{gcp_organization}

   774→[[gcp_project_number]]

   776→{gcp_project_number}::
   777→A globally unique numeric identifier automatically assigned to every
   778→{gcp_service}
   779→{gcp_project_id}.
   780→
   781→[[gcp_project_id]]
   782→// ⟦axl_voices axtg_project_id⟧
   783→{gcp_project_id}::
   784→A globally unique string identifier chosen during
   785→{gcp_project_id}
   786→creation that serves as the
   787→{gcp_project_id}
   788→'s primary reference.
   789→
   790→[[gcp_lro]]
   791→// ⟦axl_voices axig_lro⟧
   792→{gcp_lro}::
   793→A Long Running Operation, an asynchronous operation in

   796→
   797→[[gcp_delete_requested]]
   798→// ⟦axl_voices axig_project_state⟧
   799→{gcp_delete_requested}::
   800→A
   801→{gcp_project_id}
   802→lifecycle state indicating deletion has been initiated but the
   803→{gcp_project_id}
   804→remains recoverable for 30 days.
   805→
   806→[[gcp_billing_enabled]]
   807→// ⟦axl_voices axig_project_state⟧
   808→{gcp_billing_enabled}::
   809→A
   810→{gcp_project_id}
   811→status indicating that billing is active and the
   812→{gcp_project_id}
   813→can incur charges.
   814→
   815→=== Google Service Definitions
   816→
   817→[[gar_registry]]
   818→// ⟦axl_voices axig_artifact_registry⟧
   819→{gar_registry}::
   820→Google's managed service for storing and managing container images and other software artifacts.
   821→
   822→[[gcs_bucket]]
   823→// ⟦axl_voices axig_bucket⟧
   824→{gcs_bucket}::
   825→A container for storing objects in
   826→{gcs_service}
   827→with configurable access controls and lifecycle policies.
   828→
   829→[[gcb_build]]
   830→// ⟦axl_voices axig_build⟧
   831→{gcb_build}::
   832→An execution instance of
   833→{gcb_service}
   834→that processes build instructions to create artifacts.
   835→
   836→[[giam_role]]

   858→=== Bash Console Control Voicings
   859→
   860→Control terms for Recipe Bottle operations executing in a bash shell with human operator present.
   861→Each term voices the corresponding
   862→{xref_AXLA}
   863→control motif in the
   864→{axe_bash_interactive}
   865→environment.
   866→
   867→[[rbbc_variable]]
   868→// ⟦axl_voices axc_variable axe_bash_interactive⟧
   869→{rbbc_variable}::
   870→A shell variable denoted by guillemets `«NAME»` storing intermediate results.
   871→Implemented as bash variables or associative array entries.
   872→
   873→[[rbbc_fatal]]
   874→// ⟦axl_voices axc_fatal axe_bash_interactive⟧
   875→{rbbc_fatal}::
   876→Red console text followed by `exit 1`.
   877→Script terminates immediately. Human operator must intervene.
   878→
   879→[[rbbc_warn]]
   880→// ⟦axl_voices axc_warn axe_bash_interactive⟧
   881→{rbbc_warn}::
   882→Yellow console text indicating non-blocking concern.
   883→Script continues execution. Human operator may note for later.
   884→
   885→[[rbbc_store]]
   886→// ⟦axl_voices axc_store axe_bash_interactive⟧
   887→{rbbc_store}::
   888→Capture of a value from command output or API response into a
   889→{rbbc_variable}.
   890→Typically via bash assignment or jq extraction.
   891→
   892→[[rbbc_use]]
   893→// ⟦axl_voices axc_use axe_bash_interactive⟧
   894→{rbbc_use}::
   895→Reference to a previously stored
   896→{rbbc_variable}
   897→value.
   898→Expanded via bash variable substitution.
   899→
   900→[[rbbc_require]]
   901→// ⟦axl_voices axc_require axe_bash_interactive⟧
   902→{rbbc_require}::
   903→Validation check that confirms an expected condition.
   904→Triggers
   905→{rbbc_fatal}
   906→if condition not met.
   907→
   908→[[rbbc_poll]]
   909→// ⟦axl_voices axc_poll axe_bash_interactive⟧
   910→{rbbc_poll}::
   911→Repeated execution of a command at defined intervals until condition satisfied or timeout.
   912→Implemented as bash while loop with sleep.
   913→
   914→[[rbbc_call]]
   915→// ⟦axl_voices axc_call axe_bash_interactive⟧
   916→{rbbc_call}::
   917→Synchronous invocation of a REST API endpoint via curl.
   918→Captures HTTP status code and response body. Returns when response received.
   919→
   920→[[rbbc_submit]]
   921→// ⟦axl_voices axc_submit axe_bash_interactive⟧
   922→{rbbc_submit}::
   923→Asynchronous invocation of a REST API that returns an LRO operation handle.
   924→Returns immediately with operation ID for later
   925→{rbbc_await}.
   926→
   927→[[rbbc_await]]
   928→// ⟦axl_voices axc_await axe_bash_interactive⟧
   929→{rbbc_await}::
   930→Blocking wait on a submitted LRO operation until completion.
   931→Polls operation endpoint until done=true, then returns result.
   932→
   933→[[rbbc_show]]
   934→// ⟦axl_voices axc_show axe_bash_interactive⟧
   935→{rbbc_show}::
   936→Presentation of operational facts to the console.
   937→Formatted output via echo or printf to stdout.
   938→
   939→[[rbbc_prompt]]
   940→// ⟦axl_voices axc_prompt axe_bash_interactive⟧
   941→{rbbc_prompt}::
   942→Interactive request for human operator confirmation before proceeding.
   943→Displays message and waits for user input (typically y/N).
   944→Script pauses until confirmation received; continues on affirmative, triggers
   945→{rbbc_fatal}
   946→on decline or timeout.

   987→=== Recipe Bottle Orchestration Patterns (RB-specific)
   988→
   989→[[rbtoe_rbra_generate]]
   990→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
   991→{rbtoe_rbra_generate}::
   992→A pattern that converts Google
   993→{giam_service_account}
   994→credentials into
   995→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
   996→format:
   997→extracts `client_email`, `private_key`, and `project_id` from base64-encoded key data or JSON structure,
   998→converts to RBRA environment variables (`RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL`, `RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY`, `RBRA_PROJECT_ID`),
   999→and writes to specified file path with 600 permissions (owner read/write only).
  1000→
  1001→[[rbtoe_rbra_load]]
  1002→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  1003→{rbtoe_rbra_load}::
  1004→A pattern that sources an
  1005→{rbtgi_rbra_file}
  1006→and validates required fields:
  1007→sources the specified RBRA file into the current environment,
  1008→validates that `RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL`, `RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY`, and `RBRA_PROJECT_ID` are all present and non-empty,
  1009→and triggers
  1010→{rbbc_fatal}
  1011→with standardized error message if any field is missing or invalid.
  1012→
  1013→[[rbtoe_jwt_oauth_exchange]]
  1014→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  1015→{rbtoe_jwt_oauth_exchange}::
  1016→A pattern that exchanges
  1017→{giam_service_account}
  1018→RBRA credentials for an
  1019→{oauth_token}:
  1020→constructs JWT with RS256 signature using RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY,
  1021→sets JWT claims (iss=RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL, aud=https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token, scope=https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform),
  1022→POSTs to OAuth2 token endpoint,
  1023→returns access_token from response,
  1024→triggers
  1025→{rbbc_fatal}
  1026→on non-200 HTTP response.
  1027→
  1028→[[rbtoe_api_enable]]
  1029→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  1030→{rbtoe_api_enable}::
  1031→A pattern that ensures a specified Google Cloud API is enabled in a project with idempotent behavior:
  1032→
  1033→. attempts to enable the API via serviceusage.services.enable,
  1034→. tolerates HTTP 200 (success) or HTTP 400 with "already enabled" error,
  1035→. executes
  1036→{rbbc_await}
  1037→if Long Running Operation returned,
  1038→. verifies API state is ENABLED via serviceusage.services.get,
  1039→. triggers
  1040→{rbbc_fatal}
  1041→on unexpected errors (409 conflicts, permission denied, etc.),
  1042→. returns success when API is confirmed enabled.
  1043→
  1044→
  1045→[[rbtoe_oauth_refresh]]
  1046→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  1047→{rbtoe_oauth_refresh}::
  1048→A pattern that exchanges OAuth refresh token for access token:
  1049→
  1050→. loads RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN and RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET from
  1051→{rbro_regime},
  1052→. loads RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID from
  1053→{rbrp_payor_project_id}
  1054→configuration,
  1055→. constructs JSON request with refresh_token, client_id, client_secret, and grant_type=refresh_token,
  1056→. POSTs to https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token with Content-Type: application/json,
  1057→. validates HTTP 200 response or triggers
  1058→{rbbc_fatal}
  1059→with error description,
  1060→. extracts access_token from JSON response,
  1061→. {rbbc_require} access_token is present and non-empty,
  1062→. returns fresh access_token for immediate use,
  1063→. handles 400/401 errors by triggering
  1064→{rbbc_fatal}
  1065→with "OAuth credentials expired or invalid - run
  1066→{rbtgo_payor_refresh}
  1067→".
  1068→
  1069→[[rbtoe_payor_authenticate]]
  1070→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  1071→{rbtoe_payor_authenticate}::
  1072→A pattern that establishes
  1073→{rbtr_payor}
  1074→OAuth context:
  1075→
  1076→. executes
  1077→{rbtoe_rbro_load}
  1078→to load OAuth credentials,
  1079→. loads RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID from rbrp.env configuration,
  1080→. executes
  1081→{rbtoe_oauth_refresh}
  1082→to obtain access token,
  1083→. stores token as `«PAYOR_ACCESS_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls.
  1084→
  1085→[[rbtoe_governor_authenticate]]
  1086→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  1087→{rbtoe_governor_authenticate}::
  1088→A pattern that establishes
  1089→{rbtr_governor}
  1090→service account context:
  1091→
  1092→. {rbbc_require}
  1093→{rbrr_governor_rbra_file}
  1094→exists or
  1095→{rbbc_fatal}
  1096→"Governor RBRA file missing - run
  1097→{rbtgo_governor_reset}",
  1098→. loads RBRA credentials (RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL, RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY, RBRA_PROJECT_ID),
  1099→. generates JWT with scope `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform`,
  1100→. {rbbc_call} link:https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token[Google OAuth token endpoint] to exchange JWT for access token,
  1101→. stores token as `«GOVERNOR_ACCESS_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls.
  1102→
  1103→[[rbtoe_director_authenticate]]
  1104→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  1105→{rbtoe_director_authenticate}::
  1106→A pattern that establishes
  1107→{rbtr_director}
  1108→service account context:
  1109→
  1110→. {rbbc_require}
  1111→{rbrr_director_rbra_file}
  1112→exists or
  1113→{rbbc_fatal}
  1114→"Director RBRA file missing - run
  1115→{rbtgo_director_create}",
  1116→. loads RBRA credentials (RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL, RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY, RBRA_PROJECT_ID),
  1117→. generates JWT with scope `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform`,
  1118→. {rbbc_call} link:https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token[Google OAuth token endpoint] to exchange JWT for access token,
  1119→. stores token as `«DIRECTOR_ACCESS_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls.
  1120→
  1121→[[rbtoe_retriever_authenticate]]
  1122→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  1123→{rbtoe_retriever_authenticate}::
  1124→A pattern that establishes
  1125→{rbtr_retriever}
  1126→service account context:
  1127→
  1128→. {rbbc_require}
  1129→{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file}
  1130→exists or
  1131→{rbbc_fatal}
  1132→"Retriever RBRA file missing - run
  1133→{rbtgo_retriever_create}",
  1134→. loads RBRA credentials (RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL, RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY, RBRA_PROJECT_ID),
  1135→. generates JWT with scope `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform`,
  1136→. {rbbc_call} link:https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token[Google OAuth token endpoint] to exchange JWT for access token,
  1137→. stores token as `«RETRIEVER_ACCESS_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls.
  1138→
  1139→[[rbtoe_rbro_load]]
  1140→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  1141→{rbtoe_rbro_load}::
  1142→A pattern that loads and validates RBRO credentials:
  1143→
  1144→. {rbbc_require} ~/.rbw/ directory exists or
  1145→{rbbc_fatal}
  1146→"RBRO directory missing - run {rbtgo_payor_install}",
  1147→. {rbbc_require} ~/.rbw/rbro.env file exists or
  1148→{rbbc_fatal}
  1149→"RBRO credentials missing - run {rbtgo_payor_install}",
  1150→. validates file permissions are 600 or
  1151→{rbbc_warn}
  1152→"RBRO file permissions should be 600",
  1153→. sources ~/.rbw/rbro.env into current environment,
  1154→. validates RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET is present and non-empty or
  1155→{rbbc_fatal}
  1156→"RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET missing",
  1157→. validates RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN is present and non-empty or
  1158→{rbbc_fatal}
  1159→"RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN missing",
  1160→. returns success when all RBRO variables are loaded and validated.
  1161→
  1162→[[rbtoe_depot_list_update]]
  1163→// ⟦axl_voices axo_routine axe_bash_interactive⟧
  1164→{rbtoe_depot_list_update}::
  1165→A pattern that synchronizes depot project tracking:
  1166→
  1167→. executes
  1168→{rbtoe_payor_authenticate}
  1169→to obtain access token,
  1170→. {rbbc_call} link:https://cloud.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/list[projects.list] with filter `projectId:rbw-depot-* AND lifecycleState:ACTIVE`,
  1171→. {rbbc_require} HTTP 200 response from projects API,
  1172→. extracts project IDs from `.projects[].projectId` array,
  1173→. validates each project ID matches pattern `rbw-[name]-[timestamp]`,
  1174→. constructs space-delimited string from valid depot project IDs,
  1175→. updates RBRP_DEPOT_PROJECT_IDS in rbrp.env file,
  1176→. handles empty results by setting RBRP_DEPOT_PROJECT_IDS="",
  1177→. {rbbc_require} rbrp.env file update successful,
  1178→. invoked by
  1179→{rbtgo_payor_install},
  1180→{rbtgo_depot_create},
  1181→and
  1182→{rbtgo_depot_destroy}
  1183→operations.

  1264→=== Repository Regime (RBRR)
  1265→
  1266→The
  1267→regime
  1268→defines installation-wide configuration that rarely changes and is shared across all
  1269→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1270→operations.
  1271→This specification documents only the subset of RBRR variables relevant to
  1272→{gcp_service}
  1273→operations.
  1274→
  1275→[[rbrr_regime]]
  1276→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_regime axf_bash⟧
  1277→{rbrr_regime}::
  1278→An
  1279→regime
  1280→that defines repository-wide configuration and installation-wide settings shared across all
  1281→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1282→operations.
  1283→RBRR regimes contain depot project identifiers, regional settings, repository locations, and service account credential file paths that establish the foundational infrastructure configuration.

  1286→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtg_project_id⟧

  1289→{gcp_service}
  1290→{gcp_project_id}
  1291→where all
  1292→{at_recipe_bottle}
  1293→resources are created and managed.
  1294→Used as the target
  1295→{gcp_project_id}
  1296→in API calls and as the audience claim in
  1297→{oauth_token}
  1298→exchange.
